metadata.description: Curated query set with ground-truth labels for the BM25 tool-search POC. Queries span four categories: obvious (expect correct tool at rank 1), oblique (expect correct tool in top-3, tests semantic flex), adversarial (synonyms / typos / cross-tool collisions, tests robustness), recall-stress (intentionally vague, establishes floor on completely ambiguous inputs). Ground-truth `relevant_tools` is a SET of tools considered correct matches for the query — Precision@K, Recall@K, and MRR are all computed against this set.

categories.obvious: Direct queries; correct tool should rank #1. Tests baseline competence.
categories.oblique: Indirect queries that don't use the tool's literal vocabulary; correct tool should rank in top-3. Tests semantic flex via lexical overlap.
categories.adversarial: Synonyms, typos, or queries where multiple tools have legitimate claims. Tests robustness and disambiguation under collision.
categories.recall_stress: Intentionally vague or nonsensical queries. Tests that BM25 doesn't over-confidently rank irrelevant tools. Many of these have empty `relevant_tools` — the right answer is 'nothing should rank confidently.'

metadata.tool_id_format: Tools are identified by {server, tool} pairs matching the corpus.md headings. E.g., {server: 'server-filesystem', tool: 'read_text_file'} maps to corpus.md's `## Server: server-filesystem` → `### Tool: read_text_file`.

queries.query: read a text file from disk
queries.notes: Corpus also has read_file (DEPRECATED — should rank LOWER than read_text_file based on description hint), read_multiple_files, and read_media_file. BM25 may struggle to prefer the canonical over the deprecated alias.

queries.query: list all files in a directory
queries.notes: Both list_directory and list_directory_with_sizes legitimate; either at rank 1 is acceptable.

queries.query: get the current git status
show me the commit history
create a new GitHub issue
merge a pull request on GitHub
fetch the contents of a URL
take a screenshot of the current page
click on an element on the page
navigate the browser to a URL
queries.notes: Corpus also has mcp-server-browserbase/navigate — both legitimate. Top-3 should include both.

queries.query: get the YouTube transcript for a video
execute a SELECT SQL query
queries.notes: Corpus also has cloudflare-mcp/d1_database_query which is also a SQL-query tool — should be in top-3 as a legitimate alternative.

queries.query: I want to know what changed since my last commit
queries.notes: All four git_diff variants and git_status are legitimate; any of them in top-3 is a pass.

queries.query: store this information so I can find it later
queries.notes: Both memory-graph storage and filesystem write are legitimate interpretations. BM25 likely favors filesystem/write_file due to 'store' being more associated with file I/O in tool descriptions.

queries.query: I need a small embedded database for some local data
queries.notes: Any sqlite tool ranks well; this just tests that the server is identifiable from oblique terminology.

queries.query: automate filling out a form on a website
look at the source code of my Cloudflare Worker
find Terraform modules I can use in my infrastructure
connect my regional database to the Cloudflare edge
queries.notes: 'Hyperdrive' is Cloudflare's regional-DB-to-edge feature — the query describes the use case without naming the feature. BM25 will succeed only if Cloudflare's hyperdrive tool descriptions explain the feature in plain terms.

queries.query: I need to look up biomedical research
queries.notes: BioMCP is a single tool with a ~10KB description. This tests whether the massive description helps or hurts ranking.

queries.query: create a new cloud storage bucket
switch between git branches
find my notes from before
I want to make a backup of this branch first
search for code
queries.notes: Cross-server collision: GitHub has search_code, memory has search_nodes, filesystem has search_files. 'Code' should bias toward GitHub's tool.

queries.query: remove a file from my GitHub repo
queries.notes: Verb mismatch: query uses 'remove', tool uses 'delete'. Tests synonym handling. BM25 won't auto-bridge 'remove'↔'delete' without stemming or a synonym dictionary.

queries.query: find the latest release
queries.notes: Competing tools: github/list_releases, get_release_by_tag both relevant but get_latest_release is the specific match for 'latest'.

queries.query: search for issues
queries.notes: Many GitHub 'search' tools; this should disambiguate via 'issues' token.

queries.query: list all the things I have starred
queries.notes: 'Things' is intentionally vague; should test whether BM25 latches onto 'starred' as the disambiguator.

queries.query: lits files in directory
queries.notes: INTENTIONAL TYPO ('lits' for 'list'). Tests BM25 robustness to typos. Likely fails without edit-distance support — that's the data point we want.

queries.query: remove an item
queries.notes: Vague delete; many legitimate matches. Recall@K should be high; Precision@K naturally lower. Tests how BM25 handles 'large relevant set' queries.

queries.query: wait for something on the page
run a query against a database
queries.notes: Cross-server collision between SQLite and Cloudflare D1.

queries.query: open it
queries.notes: Extremely vague pronoun. Tests Recall under maximum ambiguity. Either match in top-3 is reasonable; neither at rank 1 is also acceptable.

queries.query: key-value store
queries.notes: Term 'key-value' should map to Cloudflare KV cluster directly. Tests whether BM25 picks up the multi-word term.

queries.query: I want to comment on something on GitHub
queries.notes: Multiple legitimate 'add comment' tools on GitHub. Tests recall on duplicate-ish tools.

queries.query: help me with my stuff
queries.notes: EMPTY relevant set. The right answer is 'nothing should rank confidently.' If BM25 returns high scores for anything, that's a false positive worth measuring.

queries.query: tool
queries.notes: Single-word query that matches the literal token 'tool' if it appears in descriptions. Tests how BM25 handles meta-words. Empty relevant set.

queries.query: do something useful
queries.notes: Maximally vague. Empty relevant set.

queries.query: I need a thing
queries.notes: No information content. Empty relevant set.

queries.query: manage my bitcoin wallet
queries.notes: Domain not represented in corpus. Tests that BM25 doesn't force-rank irrelevant tools. Empty relevant set; if anything ranks above a low confidence threshold, that's a false positive.

queries.query: read all files in a folder at once
show me what's in my knowledge graph
create a new GitHub repository
automate browser interactions for testing
queries.notes: Two competing browser-automation servers (Browserbase + Playwright). Tests how BM25 handles cross-server functional overlap.

queries.query: run an AWS CLI command
what's the latest version of a Terraform provider
create
queries.notes: Single verb. Highest-recall query in the set. Tests whether BM25 can return a broad-but-relevant top-K. Precision@K naturally low; Recall@K and the spread of servers in top-K is the signal.
